new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 l (Unterposition 2208 50 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin, being an alcoholic beverage generally produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by simple or successive distillation with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The primary distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 50 19 are: (1) the product is gin – an alcoholic beverage produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by distillation with juniper berries and other aromatic substances; (2) it is presented in containers holding MORE than 2 litres. These two criteria are captured via: subClassOf Gin2208 (gin identity), someValuesFrom LargeContainer (container > 2 L), someValuesFrom GinDistillationProcess (production method), and someValuesFrom JuniperBerryIngredient (key ingredient). A disjointness complement from GinSmallContainer22085011 (≤ 2 L) is also expressed. The legal note text is truncated and does not provide a full enumeration of all aromatic substances or quantitative alcohol strength thresholds, so the coverage is not 1.0. The RectifiedCerealSpirit ingredient restriction (alternative base spirit) is not separately axiomatised to avoid over-committing on the disjunction with ethyl alcohol as base; this is a minor gap. The complement restriction uses owl_class_iri incorrectly (should be on the new class itself as a disjointness). Overall coverage is approximately 0.85.
